rule.name: Ng vs Nang with Adjectives
rule.Description: Detects incorrect use of "ng" before adjectives that are not followed by a noun. "ng" should introduce a noun or noun phrase. If an adjective follows "ng" without a noun, "nang" should be used instead. "nang" is correct when it modifies a verb (adverbial use).

Examples.Correct: Gusto niya ng masarap na pagkain.
Bumili siya ng mga magagandang damit.

Check.message: Gamitin ang 'nang' kapag nagbibigay-uring pang-abay sa pandiwa (hal., 'nang mabilis', 'nang maayos'). Use 'nang' when the word after it is an adjective modifying a verb.

rule.Suggestion:
Palitan ang "ng" ng "nang" kapag ang susunod na salita ay pang-uri lamang na nagbibigay-uring pang-abay sa pandiwa. Panatilihin ang "ng" kapag nagpapakilala ito ng pangngalan o pariralang pangngalan (hal., 'ng magandang damit', 'ng malaking kahon').
Replace "ng" with "nang" if the word after it is only an adjective modifying a verb. Keep "ng" if it introduces a noun phrase (e.g., 'ng magandang damit', 'ng malaking kahon').
rule.Notes: - "Nang" is also used before verbs in subordinate clauses (e.g., "nang dumating siya") — this rule does not flag those cases. - Reduplication forms (e.g., "dahan-dahan", "biglaan") are included in the adjective/adverb list. - This rule focuses on adjective-only adverbial use; noun phrases with adjective modifiers are allowed. - "ng mga" is always valid and is never flagged. "Mga" is the plural marker and always introduces a noun phrase (e.g., "ng mga damit", "ng mga magagandang bulaklak").
